name: Salmon Dilly
recipeInstructions:
This is the easiest way I know to cook tender and
delicate salmon. It's especially good prepared with
fresh dill, but if you prefer chives or basil, it's
still a delightful dish.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Place salmon in shallow baking
dish and add half-and-half or milk so fish is half
immersed. Sprinkle with dill, chives, or basil, and
salt if desired. Bake for 30 minutes, spooning some
of the half-and-half or milk over the salmon as needed.
Check for doneness; when the fish is predominantly pale
pink but remains a darker pink in the center, it's done.
Makes one serving.
Nutrients: calories 563, protein 50.9 grams, fat 36
grams, cholesterol 204 mg, fiber 0.02 grams, sodium
306 mg.
